_meta.description: Curated ground-truth bibliographic metadata for D:\bibwizard\literature\. Built by reading each PDF's embedded metadata + page-1 text. Use bibwizard/benchmark/compare.py to diff this against bibwizard's database.
_meta.notes: Author lists are in byline order. Years are publication year (or arXiv submission year for preprints). DOI / arxiv_id only when explicitly present in the document. confidence='high' means PDF embedded metadata or first-page text was unambiguous; 'medium' means I inferred from page-1 text without an authoritative metadata source.

papers.title: Direct imaging of nonsolar planets with infrared telescopes using apodized coronagraphs
papers.summary: Examines modified Lyot coronagraphs with apodized square apertures (monolithic and segmented) for IR direct imaging of extrasolar planets. Considers segment piston/tilt errors and proposes a misalignment-correction system. Conclusion: a 14.2m square objective with a cross-shaped apodizer in the first focal plane and a Lyot stop can in principle image Earth-like planets in the 10.5-13.5 μm band.

papers.title: Design of a low-loss off-axis beam expander
papers.summary: Technical note showing that astigmatism can be exactly compensated in a two-spherical-mirror off-axis beam expander by choosing the correct pair of incidence angles, plus a method to minimize losses. Aimed at precision laser experiments (e.g. Virgo) where antireflection-coating feedback rules out refractive optics.

papers.title: Extrasolar Planet Finding via Optimal Apodized-Pupil and Shaped-Pupil Coronagraphs
papers.summary: Compares apodized-pupil and shaped-pupil coronagraphs for high-contrast direct imaging of extrasolar planets. Uses numerical optimization to design new shaped-pupil masks (binary 0/1 transmission) that achieve 10^-10 contrast at inner working angles ~2.8 λ/D with throughput comparable to the best smooth apodizers, and demonstrates designs for square, rectangular, circular, and elliptical apertures.

papers.title: Exoplanet Imaging with a Phase-Induced Amplitude Apodization Coronagraph. I. Principle
papers.summary: Introduces the Phase-Induced Amplitude Apodization Coronagraph (PIAAC) concept: two aspheric mirrors remap the entrance beam losslessly to produce an apodized output pupil, retaining full throughput and angular resolution. Demonstrates via ray tracing that a 4 m PIAAC can detect Earth-sized planets at 10 pc in under 30 s, with inner working angle ~1.5 λ/D.

papers.title: Off-axis Gaussian beam intensity in random medium
papers.summary: Formulates the receiver-plane intensity profile of an off-axis Gaussian beam propagating through atmospheric turbulence. Shows that asymmetric off-axis Gaussians convert to circular shape at intermediate link lengths before re-expanding into ellipses along the orthogonal transverse axis. Relevant to horizontal optical-communication links.

papers.title: Design of the second generation PIAA system
papers.summary: Internal design document for the second-generation Phase-Induced Amplitude Apodization (PIAA) coronagraph system. Covers choice of apodization function, sharing apodization between PIAA mirrors and conventional apodizers, on-axis and off-axis mirror shape derivations, and the numerical simulation pipeline used to verify performance in the 0.5-0.8 μm band.

papers.title: Design, analysis, and testing of a microdot apodizer for the Apodized Pupil Lyot Coronagraph
papers.summary: Designs, manufactures, and laboratory-tests a microdot (binary halftone) apodizer for the Apodized Pupil Lyot Coronagraph, targeted at ELT-class instruments like SPHERE/GPI/EPICS. Transmission profile agrees with the theoretical apodization to within 3% and is achromatic; an error-diffusion algorithm chooses pixel size to control high-frequency noise.

papers.title: Numerical propagator through PIAA optics
papers.summary: Derives a quadratic-approximation angular-spectrum propagator for pupil remapping in PIAA coronagraphs. Lets numerical propagation accurately capture hard-edge diffraction effects that limit contrast, and enables a fast mid-spatial-frequency propagator suitable for wavefront-control loops with DMs upstream of the PIAA optics.

papers.title: Phase-Induced Amplitude Apodization on centrally obscured pupils: design and first laboratory demonstration for the Subaru Telescope pupil
papers.summary: First laboratory demonstration of a PIAA-type coronagraph designed for a pupil with central obscuration and spider vanes (the Subaru Telescope geometry). Uses aspheric PIAA lenses plus a Spider Removal Plate to apodize a complex pupil, achieves coronagraphic imaging as close as ~1 λ/D, and includes an inverse-PIAA stage to recover a wide field of view. Will feed the SCExAO system.

papers.title: "Modal Noise" in Single-Mode Fibers: A Cautionary Note for High Precision Radial Velocity Instruments
papers.summary: Cautionary note: even single-mode fibers, which eliminate the classical multi-mode modal noise of MMFs, propagate two orthogonal polarization modes. Mechanical stresses on the fiber induce variable birefringence, which couples through polarization-sensitive diffraction gratings to produce m/s-level RV systematics relevant to modern diffraction-limited precision-RV spectrographs (iLocater, MINERVA-Red, etc).

papers.title: Generating a Bessel-Gaussian beam for the application in optical engineering
papers.summary: Method to generate high-power Bessel-Gaussian beams by coherent combining of 16 Gaussian beams, with a discontinuous piston-phase trick to form the vortex phase. Compares intensity evolution of the combined beam against an analytic BG beam at multiple propagation distances, and discusses feasibility from existing coherent-combining experimental hardware.

papers.title: Arrays of Gaussian vortex, Bessel and Airy beams by computer-generated hologram
papers.summary: Generates rectangular, diamond, and hexagonal lattices of Gaussian vortex, Bessel, and Airy beams using digital phase holograms based on the fractional-Talbot effect on a phase-only SLM. Studies linear and nonlinear propagation of the resulting beam arrays in SBN photorefractive crystal, finding that Bessel and Airy arrays remain non-diffracting over >20 mm.

papers.title: Efficiently feeding single-mode fiber photonic spectrographs with an extreme adaptive optics system: on-sky characterization and preliminary spectroscopy
papers.summary: Construction and on-sky characterization of an efficient single-mode-fiber injection unit behind Subaru/SCExAO, fed by extreme adaptive optics + pupil apodization. Demonstrates the fiber feed for compact photonic spectrographs and presents preliminary spectra. Coupling efficiency limited mainly by the residual Strehl after ExAO correction.

papers.title: Efficient injection from large telescopes into single-mode fibres: Enabling the era of ultra-precision astronomy
papers.summary: Demonstrates that with careful pupil-apodization and extreme-AO control, light from a large telescope can be injected into a single-mode fibre with 86% of the theoretical coupling-efficiency limit at 1550 nm in the lab, and ~50% on-sky behind SCExAO with 60% Strehl. Opens single-mode photonic technologies (precision RV, interferometry, AWG spectrographs) to large-telescope instrumentation.

papers.title: Status of the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer Phase II Development
papers.summary: Reports the design and Caltech-testbed status of KPIC Phase II — a series of upgrades to the Keck II adaptive-optics + NIRC2/NIRSPEC suite for High-Dispersion Coronagraphy. Phase II adds a 952-actuator DM, PIAA lenses, an atmospheric-dispersion compensator, multiple coronagraphs, a Zernike WFS, and multiple science ports, targeting deployment in early 2020.

papers.title: Mitigation of Polarization Effects in Single-mode Fiber Spectrographs
papers.venue: Publications of the Astronomical Society of the Pacific, 132, 095001
papers.summary: Characterizes variable fiber birefringence in single-mode fibres at the lab and the LBT using Stokes-parameter measurements, then models how variable polarization couples through diffraction-grating optics to produce m/s-level RV systematics. Demonstrates that polarization-averaging methods and in-line depolarizers can knock several m/s of polarization noise down to ~10 cm/s.

papers.title: Designing a high-efficiency coupling system to couple a hollow Gaussian beam into single-mode fiber
papers.summary: Designs a two-element shaping + coupling lens system that converts the hollow Gaussian beam emerging from a Cassegrain antenna back into a solid Gaussian and couples it into a single-mode fibre. Improves coupling efficiency from ~39% (direct injection) to 81.45% in simulation (74.73% with manufacturing imperfections).

papers.title: Direct characterization of young giant exoplanets at high spectral resolution by coupling SPHERE and CRIRES+
papers.summary: End-to-end simulations of the proposed HiRISE fiber coupling between VLT/SPHERE and CRIRES+ for high-dispersion characterization of directly-imaged exoplanets. Finds HiRISE provides up to ×16 speedup over CRIRES+ for companions around bright (H<5) hosts, and outperforms CRIRES+ in 2h integrations for hosts with H<8.5 over 50-350 mas separation. The dominant performance lever is overall transmission.

papers.title: Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer: a dedicated single-mode fiber injection unit for high-resolution exoplanet spectroscopy
papers.venue: Journal of Astronomical Telescopes, Instruments, and Systems, 7, 035006
papers.summary: Describes the design, calibration, and on-sky performance of the KPIC fiber injection unit at Keck Observatory. The FIU sits downstream of Keck II AO and couples starlight + companion light into single-mode fibres feeding NIRSPEC at R>30,000, enabling high-dispersion coronagraphy on directly-imaged exoplanets and brown dwarfs.

papers.title: Final design and on-sky testing of the iLocater SX acquisition camera: broad-band single-mode fibre coupling
papers.venue: Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 501, 2250
papers.summary: Final design and on-sky validation of the SX acquisition camera for the iLocater diffraction-limited spectrograph on the Large Binocular Telescope. Provides broad-band single-mode fibre coupling and high-precision target acquisition for the iLocater dual-channel spectrograph.

papers.title: Enhancing Direct Exoplanet Spectroscopy with Apodizing and Beam Shaping Optics
papers.venue: Publications of the Astronomical Society of the Pacific, 133, 024503
papers.summary: Studies how PIAA-style apodizing and beam-shaping optics improve fiber-coupling efficiency for direct exoplanet spectroscopy in KPIC and similar instruments. Quantifies the throughput gains achievable with PIAA lenses ahead of the fiber-injection unit.

papers.title: Model validation of Phase-Induced Amplitude Apodization Complex Mask Coronagraph for LUVOIR-A in vacuum
papers.summary: Laboratory and simulation validation of the PIAA Complex Mask Coronagraph (PIAACMC) for the LUVOIR-A on-axis segmented architecture. Demonstrates contrast performance in vacuum testbed and compares against end-to-end propagation models.
papers.notes: Verified from page-1 byline. Year=2021 per filename and SPIE volume — bibwizard's 2023 finding is the print year.

papers.title: Fundamental limit of single-mode integral-field spectroscopy
papers.summary: Derives the fundamental performance limit of single-mode integral-field spectroscopy under diffraction-limited ground-based observation. Establishes how single-mode IFS coupling efficiency scales with adaptive-optics Strehl ratio and informs design trade-offs for ELT-era single-mode IFS instruments.

papers.title: Validation of strategies for coupling exoplanet PSFs into single-mode fibres for high-dispersion coronagraphy
papers.summary: Laboratory validation of alternative strategies (different fiber-injection unit architectures and stellar-light suppression schemes) for coupling exoplanet PSFs into single-mode fibres for High-Dispersion Coronagraphy with the HiRISE/SPHERE-CRIRES+ system. Compares achievable performance against end-to-end simulations.

papers.title: Fiber-Fed High-Resolution Infrared Spectroscopy at the diffraction limit with Keck-HISPEC and TMT-MODHIS: status update
papers.summary: Status update on Keck-HISPEC and TMT-MODHIS, the next-generation diffraction-limited fiber-fed high-resolution near-infrared spectrographs for Keck and TMT. Covers science drivers (RV characterization, exoplanet atmospheres), instrument design, and development progress as of the 2022 SPIE proceedings.

papers.title: Design, manufacturing, and testing of phase-induced amplitude apodization and phase-shifting optics for segmented telescopes
papers.summary: Designs, manufactures, and laboratory-tests PIAA + phase-shifting optics optimized for the segmented pupils of future giant telescopes (ELT/TMT/GMT). Demonstrates manufacturing feasibility of the complex aspheric profiles and characterizes the resulting beam apodization on a testbed.

papers.title: Characterizing hot Jupiter Atmospheres with Keck/KPIC
papers.summary: Reports KPIC observations of hot-Jupiter atmospheres at high spectral resolution (R~35,000) in the K band, using high-dispersion coronagraphy to measure orbital velocities and atmospheric molecular composition. Discusses sensitivity, signal-to-noise, and the population of accessible targets.

papers.title: RISTRETTO: the PIAA Nuller in the prototyping phase
papers.summary: Reports the prototyping status of the RISTRETTO PIAA Nuller — an extreme-AO + nulling coronagraph designed for Proxima Cen and similar nearby M-dwarf systems, aiming at very small inner working angles (~2 λ/D). Covers IFU bundle characterization, first PIAA optics, and integration of the high-contrast bench.

papers.title: Technical description and performance of the phase II version of the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer
papers.venue: Journal of Astronomical Telescopes, Instruments, and Systems, 11, 015005
papers.summary: Detailed technical description and on-sky performance of Phase II of the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer (KPIC). Phase II adds new wavefront-control hardware, PIAA optics, and an improved fiber-injection unit to enable high-dispersion coronagraphy at higher contrast and inner working angle than Phase I. Reports throughput, contrast, and the science demonstrations through 2024.

papers.title: Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer: concept and phased implementation
papers.summary: Introduces the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer (KPIC) concept and outlines its three-phase implementation plan. Phase I delivers a NIR pyramid wavefront sensor and a fiber injection unit linking Keck-AO to NIRSPEC; Phase II adds PIAA optics, coronagraphs, and additional WFS modules; Phase III explores further upgrades.

papers.title: A fiber injection unit for the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer
papers.summary: Detailed design of the Phase-I fiber injection unit (FIU) for KPIC: optics layout, tracking-camera subsystem, single-mode fiber routing from the AO bench to NIRSPEC, and predicted throughput. The FIU was the first KPIC subsystem installed at the W. M. Keck Observatory in 2018.

papers.title: The Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer: Phase I fiber injection unit early performance and commissioning
papers.summary: Reports the on-sky commissioning and early-performance characterization of the KPIC Phase-I fiber injection unit. Measures the end-to-end throughput, contrast at the planet location, and stability of the FIU, demonstrating the system's readiness for high-dispersion coronagraphy.

papers.title: The high-contrast performance of the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer
papers.summary: Quantifies the on-sky high-contrast performance of KPIC at Keck — fiber-injection efficiency, raw contrast, and post-processed sensitivity at small inner working angles. Reports the planets and brown dwarfs detected to date and outlines KPIC's contribution to the inner-working-angle frontier of high-dispersion coronagraphy.

papers.title: Technical description and performance of the phase II version of the Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer
papers.summary: SPIE-proceedings companion paper to the JATIS 2025 article — technical description of KPIC Phase II covering the new DM, PIAA optics, ADC, coronagraphs, ZWFS, and multiple science ports, plus on-sky performance results. Same instrument, different venue, partial author overlap.
papers.notes: Verified from page-1 byline read. Same instrument as 2025_JATIS_Jovanovic_* but separate SPIE-proceedings paper.

papers.title: Keck Planet Imager and Characterizer: a dedicated single-mode fiber injection unit for high-resolution exoplanet spectroscopy
papers.venue: Journal of Astronomical Telescopes, Instruments, and Systems, 7, 035006
papers.summary: Likely identical to the 2021_JATIS_Delorme_* entry — same title and primary author. Kept as a separate record because the file lives separately on disk and has its own SHA-256.
papers.notes: Almost certainly the same paper as 2021_JATIS_Delorme_*.pdf. bibwizard's SHA-256 dedup should catch this on ingest.

papers.title: Gaussian Beams: Optical Design Essentials
papers.summary: Web archive (apparently a print-to-PDF of an optical-design blog or tutorial page) on Gaussian beam basics for optical design. Not a refereed publication — heuristic scrapers will fail to extract sensible metadata because the document has no proper byline. Tagging it gaussian-beams / optical-design and removing it from the library is reasonable.
papers.notes: NOT A REAL PAPER. Title and author fields are best-effort. Recommend removing this from the library.
